title: psydrax subcordatus
intro: Psydrax subcordatus, also known as the Madagascar periwinkle's relative, is a flowering plant species native to Madagascar. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and stems, which contain compounds that exhibit antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Psydrax subcordatus include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. In traditional medicine, Psydrax subcordatus is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of health conditions.
